items.title: Disable SSH Root Login
items.description: Prevent direct root login via SSH to reduce attack surface.
items.impact: Unauthorized root access, complete system compromise.

implementation.linux:
# Edit /etc/ssh/sshd_config:
PermitRootLogin no
PasswordAuthentication no
PubkeyAuthentication yes

# Restart SSH:
systemctl restart sshd

mitigationSteps: 1. Create non-root user with sudo access
7. Test login in new session before closing current

items.title: Configure Host-based Firewall
items.description: Enable and configure UFW or firewalld with default-deny policy.
items.threat: Network Intrusion - Unfiltered ports accessible to attackers.
items.impact: Unauthorized access to services, lateral movement.

implementation.linux:
# Using UFW:
ufw default deny incoming
ufw default allow outgoing
ufw allow 80/tcp
ufw allow 443/tcp
ufw allow 22/tcp
ufw enable

# Check status:
ufw status verbose

testing: nmap shows filtered ports for others

items.title: Enable Automatic Security Updates
items.description: Configure unattended-upgrades to automatically install security patches.
items.threat: Known Vulnerabilities - Unpatched systems vulnerable to exploits.
items.rollback: Uninstall unattended-upgrades or disable in config.

items.title: Install and Configure Fail2Ban
items.description: Block IP addresses with multiple failed authentication attempts.
items.threat: Brute Force Attacks - Repeated login attempts.

implementation.linux:
# Install:
apt install fail2ban

# Configure /etc/fail2ban/jail.local:
[DEFAULT]
bantime = 3600
findtime = 600
maxretry = 3

[sshd]
enabled = true

[apache-auth]
enabled = true

items.title: Enable System Auditing with auditd
items.description: Monitor and log security-relevant events on the system.
items.threat: Undetected Intrusions - Without auditing, attacks go unnoticed.
items.impact: Delayed breach detection, no forensic evidence.

implementation.linux:
# Install:
apt install auditd audispd-plugins

# Configure /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules:
-w /etc/passwd -p wa -k identity
-w /etc/group -p wa -k identity
-w /etc/shadow -p wa -k identity
-w /etc/sudoers -p wa -k sudoers
-w /var/www/html -p wa -k web_changes

items.title: Enable AppArmor or SELinux
items.description: Mandatory Access Control (MAC) to restrict application capabilities.
items.threat: Privilege Escalation, Lateral Movement - Compromised applications have full access.

implementation.linux:
# For AppArmor (Ubuntu/Debian):
apt install apparmor apparmor-profiles apparmor-utils

# Check status:
aa-status

# Set to enforcing:
systemctl enable apparmor

# For SELinux (RHEL/CentOS):
setenforce 1
# Edit /etc/selinux/config:
SELINUX=enforcing

items.rollback: Set to complain mode or disable.

items.title: Enforce Strong Password Policy
items.description: Configure password complexity, expiration, and history requirements.
items.threat: Weak Credentials - Simple passwords can be cracked.

implementation.linux:
# Edit /etc/security/pwquality.conf:
minlen = 12
minclass = 3
maxrepeat = 2
reject_username

# Edit /etc/login.defs:
PASS_MAX_DAYS 90
PASS_MIN_DAYS 7
PASS_WARN_AGE 14

# For existing users:
chage -M 90 -m 7 -W 14 username

mitigationSteps: 4. Apply to existing users with chage

items.title: Secure Critical File Permissions
items.description: Set proper ownership and permissions on critical system files.
items.threat: Privilege Escalation - Weak permissions allow unauthorized modifications.

implementation.linux:
# Fix shadow files:
chmod 640 /etc/shadow
chown root:shadow /etc/shadow

# Fix passwd/group:
chmod 644 /etc/passwd /etc/group
chown root:root /etc/passwd /etc/group

# Fix sudoers:
chmod 440 /etc/sudoers
chown root:root /etc/sudoers

# Find SUID/SGID binaries:
find / -perm /6000 -type f

# Find world-writable files:
find / -xdev -type f -perm -0002

items.title: Secure Kernel Parameters
items.description: Configure sysctl parameters for enhanced security.
items.threat: Network Attacks, IP Spoofing - Default kernel settings may be permissive.

implementation.linux:
# Edit /etc/sysctl.conf or /etc/sysctl.d/99-security.conf:

# IP Spoofing protection:
net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1
net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter = 1

# Disable source routing:
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0

# Disable ICMP redirects:
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0

# Enable TCP SYN cookies:
net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies = 1

# Apply:
sysctl -p

items.rollback: Comment out lines in sysctl config.

items.title: Secure Log Files
items.description: Protect log files from tampering and unauthorized access.
items.threat: Log Tampering - Attackers may delete evidence.

implementation.linux:
# Set proper permissions:
chmod 640 /var/log/auth.log
chmod 640 /var/log/syslog
chown root:adm /var/log/*.log

# Configure logrotation:
# /etc/logrotate.d/auth:
/var/log/auth.log {
    daily
    missingok
    rotate 30
    compress
    delaycompress
    notifempty
    create 640 root adm
}

mitigationSteps: 1. Set correct permissions on log files
testing: Normal users cannot read sensitive logs
